artifact: IEC 62304 Traceability Matrix (variant C: concrete evidence)

rows.requirement_text: Bolus request exceeding the maximum permissible dose limit shall trigger a Dose limit exceeded alarm. (GIP v1.0 Safety Requirement 1.4.12)
rows.caveat: Holds for the specific recorded inputs only; no claim of generality beyond them.

rows.requirement_text: Continuous reverse delivery shall not be possible during normal use or a single-fault condition. Modelled at this kernel as a fault input: a negative infusion rate (hardware reverse-flow fault) shall yield exactly zero delivered dose. (GIP v1.0 Safety Requirement 1.8.1, citing IEC 601-2-24; fault modelling added in the 2026-07-04 Option A amendment)
rows.caveat: Holds for the specific recorded inputs only; no claim of generality beyond them.

rows.requirement_text: Continuous reverse delivery shall not be possible during normal use or a single-fault condition. Modelled at this kernel as a fault input: a negative infusion rate (hardware reverse-flow fault) shall yield exactly zero delivered dose. (GIP v1.0 Safety Requirement 1.8.1, citing IEC 601-2-24; fault modelling added in the 2026-07-04 Option A amendment)
rows.caveat: Holds for the specific recorded inputs only; no claim of generality beyond them.

rows.requirement_text: Calculation shall yield a finite, in-range result for all inputs meeting preconditions. DECLARED - not sourced from GIP v1.0, which does not address floating-point overflow at the implementation level.
rows.caveat: Holds for the specific recorded inputs only; no claim of generality beyond them.
